title: P1 polish：72h 圖 3 小時網格正規化＋區塊標題顯示鄉鎮名
depends_on: cathay-cloud-intern-p1-date-tz-and-horizon-fix (must be accepted first; do not run concurrently on the same worktree)
gate: GATE SATISFIED 2026-07-04: date-tz-and-horizon-fix accepted (approved, HEAD 67c10ef). Cleared for execution by management.

context.background: User testing with live data exposed a chart-rendering defect the original 72h card did not anticipate: the CWA near-term feed returns MIXED granularity — roughly the first 48h at 1-hour steps, then 3-hour steps (observed 56 slices over 4 days). The chart assumes uniform 3-hourly spacing and draws a label + weather icon for every point, so the hourly segment renders as overlapping unreadable labels/icons. Separately, the user noted that after scrolling down to the chart the township context is lost — the section should carry the place name.
context.screenshot_evidence: 2026-07-04 01:23 screenshot: x-axis showed per-hour ticks 00:01:02... for 7/4-7/5 then 3-hourly for 7/6-7/7, with icons and percent labels colliding.

in_scope: P1 normalize the hourly series to a uniform 3-hour grid before rendering: aggregate or sample 1-hourly slices into 3-hour buckets (temperature: value at bucket start or mean; PoP: max within bucket; weather icon: bucket-representative code) — do this in the backend normalization or a frontend util, whichever keeps the schema honest, and document the choice in the Task Report

P2 the chart section header shows the queried place context, e.g. 縣市＋鄉鎮 (新北市 貢寮區), so the location remains visible after scrolling; keep it in sync with the current query result (not the form's in-progress selection)
P3 label/icon density guard: even on a uniform grid, if point count exceeds what fits, thin labels/icons to every Nth point while keeping curves full-resolution
P4 tests: mixed-granularity payload (1h + 3h) normalizes to the expected uniform grid; deterministic mock still renders unchanged

requirements: R1: No heavy chart library added; stay inline-SVG
R2: Tolerant parsing preserved; mock mode end-to-end intact
R3: Do not regress anything shipped by the previous cards (368 towns, two-stage form, chips, target-date advice, sunrise/UV, stable week + chart behavior from the tz-fix card)
acceptance_criteria: AC1: ruff + pytest + npm run build pass
AC2: Live near-term query (travel date today/tomorrow) renders a readable chart: uniform 3h ticks, no overlapping labels/icons
AC3: Chart section displays the queried 縣市＋鄉鎮 name and updates on a new query
AC4: Mock mode chart unchanged in structure and still deterministic
AC5: Changes committed per-AC and pushed to origin
operational_notes: Live-mode town codes are CWA-sourced (e.g. cwa-65000260); read from live /api/towns before manual testing.
This card runs AFTER the date-tz-and-horizon-fix card; rebase expectations on that card's merged week+hourly response shape.

report_template.location: Ubuntu agent-bridge reports path or repo-local equivalent used by the current workflow

user_one_liner: After the tz-fix card is accepted: normalize the mixed 1h/3h CWA slices to a uniform 3-hour grid so the 72h chart stays readable, add the queried county+township name to the chart section header, and thin labels when density demands.
next_task_hint: Remaining backlog after this: Gemini activation, MOENV AQI, actual cloud deploy (waiting on deploy auth).
